title: Stelis lanceolata
taxonomy: Stelis lanceolata is a species of orchid in the genus Stelis. This genus is part of the tribe Maxillarieae, which is a subfamily of the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The orchid family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants, with over 30,000 known species. The classification of Stelis lanceolata is further divided into higher taxonomic ranks, including kingdom, phylum, and class. Understanding the classification of Stelis lanceolata helps scientists and researchers study and understand the plant's evolutionary history and relationships with other organisms.

intro: Stelis lanceolata, also known as Lance-leafed Stelis, is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, characterized by its lance-shaped leaves and delicate white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to aid in reducing pain and improving wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Stelis lanceolata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Stelis lanceolata is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is commonly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
